channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chanel releases ad starring Margot Robbie and Kylie Minogue, directed by Michel Gondry
item.description: Chanel has launched a new advertising campaign featuring Australian actress Margot Robbie and singer Kylie Minogue. The ad, directed by acclaimed music video director Michel Gondry, was filmed on a ca...

item.title: PRASA Plans Limited Long-Distance Train Services Amid Funding Challenges
item.description: The long-distance passenger train service, Shosholoza Meyl, faces an uncertain future as PRASA announces plans to resume limited operations on four key routes in 2027. The service, which runs between ...

item.title: Long-term impact of the Ras Laffan attacks on global gas markets
item.description: On 19 March 2026, the Ras Laffan industrial complex in Qatar, the world’s largest liquefied natural gas (LNG) terminal, was severely damaged by Iranian missile and drone strikes. The attack caused ext...

item.title: Avalanche near B.C.-Alaska border kills one, four others rescued unharmed
item.description: A fatal avalanche occurred on Sunday near Pleasant Camp and the Klehini River, close to the B.C.-Alaska border. The B.C. RCMP reported receiving a satellite SOS signal indicating an avalanche, with on...

item.title: Toronto police restrict protests in Jewish residential neighbourhood amid safety concerns
item.description: Toronto police have announced a new measure preventing anti-Israel demonstrators from entering residential areas near Bathurst Street and Sheppard Avenue, a key part of the city’s Jewish community. Th...

item.title: Trump Delays Threat Against Iran Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has postponed by five days his initial deadline to target Iran's power stations and energy infrastructure, which was conditional on Tehran allowing free passage of shipping t...

item.title: Private investigator disputes claim he changed stance in Daily Mail privacy case
item.description: A private investigator, Gavin Burrows, has told the High Court that he did not 'switch sides' out of revenge in the ongoing privacy case against the publisher of the Daily Mail. The case involves high...

item.title: Two pilots, including Quebec resident, die in LaGuardia Airport crash
item.description: A tragic accident occurred Sunday night at New York's LaGuardia Airport, claiming the lives of two pilots, including Quebec resident Antoine Forest from Coteau-du-Lac, Que. Forest was employed by Jazz...

item.title: Two Indian LPG Tankers Successfully Cross Strait of Hormuz Amid US-Israel-Iran War
item.description: Two Indian-flagged LPG tankers, Jag Vasant and Pine Gas, carrying around 92,600 tonnes of cooking gas, safely crossed the war-torn Strait of Hormuz on Monday evening. The shipping ministry stated that...

item.title: Retired Police and Ex-Servicemen Invited to Assist in Tamil Nadu Assembly Election
item.description: The Tamil Nadu Police Department has called for applications from retired police officers, former constables, ex-servicemen, and ex-paramilitary personnel who are willing to assist in election-related...

item.title: Filipino students from Tarlac and Masbate recognized as Most Promising Young Scientists in SEAMEO competition
item.description: Filipino high school students from Tarlac National High School and Cataingan National High School in Masbate were named among the Most Promising Young Scientists in Southeast Asia after winning top ho...

item.title: Global effort needed to reduce tuberculosis risk for all
item.description: In this article, Alemnew Dagnew highlights the ongoing global challenge of tuberculosis (TB), stressing that the risk of catching TB should not depend on where a person is born. In high-income countri...

item.title: Zamfara Education Commissioner Madawaki Resigns Following Directive on 2027 Aspirants
item.description: Wadatau Madawaki, wey be Commissioner for Education, Science and Technology for Zamfara State, don resign from him position, and Governor Dauda Lawal don accept the resignation. According to statement...

item.title: Investigation ongoing after Negros Occidental radio journalist killed in broad daylight
item.description: Julito “Jaz” Calo, a radio blocktimer from La Castellana in Negros Occidental, was shot dead on March 20, 2026, while watering plants in front of his home. A male gunman approached him from a white SU...

item.title: Dangote Refinery boosts exports as fuel imports fall due to Middle East conflict
item.description: Nigeria's Dangote Petroleum Refinery imesimama kwa nguvu sasa, ikiongeza mauzo ya mafuta barani Africa baada ya vita vya Iran kuathiri usambazaji wa nishati. Data inaonyesha kwamba mauzo ya bidhaa saf...

item.title: Markets React to Trump’s Short-Lived De-escalation on Iran
item.description: Shortly after former US President Donald Trump announced on Truth Social that he would pause his threat to strike Iran's energy infrastructure, global markets experienced extreme volatility. Oil price...

item.title: Netanyahu affirms Israel's military actions and dialogue with Trump over Iran
item.description: On March 23, 2026, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed that he had a discussion with U.S. President Donald Trump regarding the ongoing military situation in Iran. According to Netanyah...

item.title: Kenya names provisional squad with returning players ahead of 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations
item.description: So basically, Kenya’s national women’s volleyball team, the Malkia Strikers, is preparing early for the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations, which will be hosted right here in Nairobi at Kasarani. The ...

item.title: KCB Rugby Looks Ahead to Kenya Cup Playoffs Following Tight Loss to Black Pirates
item.description: KCB Rugby suffered a narrow 11-10 defeat to Black Pirates in the Enterprise Cup quarterfinal played at Muteesa II Stadium, Kampala. The game started cautiously, with Pirates missing an early penalty, ...

item.title: Delta Sports Commission Chairman denies assault allegation, alleges blackmail plot
item.description: Chairman of Delta State Sports Commission, Onoriode Oborevwori, don come out strongly to deny allegation say him beat one kickboxing coach, Steve Momah, for him office. He talk say the story na pure b...
